\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Triangular surface element with cached geometry and utilities. 

Stores node pointers and bordering domains, computes and caches\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item reference unit normal (pointing to FRONT),
\item surface area and perimeter,
\item centroid (center of gravity),
\item analytic integrals I1, I2s, I2d.
\end{DoxyItemize}

Notes\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Node accessors return by value (Node) or pointer (Node\+Ptr).
\item \doxylink{classTriangle_a691ff1d8d3f33df1fe0aae161d14ec49}{FBNormal\+Vector()} flips the reference normal for BACK. 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{Triangle_8h_source_l00032}{32}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{Triangle_8h_source}{Triangle.\+h}}.

\Hypertarget{classTriangle_ae13d69e28fb7a81ab8257fe0af84ceaa}\index{Triangle@{Triangle}!CalculateGeometryData@{CalculateGeometryData}}
\index{CalculateGeometryData@{CalculateGeometryData}!Triangle@{Triangle}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{CalculateGeometryData()}{CalculateGeometryData()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classTriangle_ae13d69e28fb7a81ab8257fe0af84ceaa} 
int Triangle\+::\+Calculate\+Geometry\+Data (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Compute/cached geometry (normal, area, perimeter, center, I1/\+I2s/\+I2d). 

\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
0 on success.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Recompute normal (FRONT), area, perimeter, centroid, and I1/\+I2s/\+I2d.

Normal and area\+:  $ \mathbf{n} = (\mathbf{n_2}-\mathbf{n_1}) \times (\mathbf{n_3}-\mathbf{n_1}), 
  A = |\mathbf{n}|/2, \hat{\mathbf{n}} = \mathbf{n}/|\mathbf{n}| $.

Perimeter and centroid\+:  $ \mathbf{r_{cnt}} = (\mathbf{n_1}+\mathbf{n_2}+\mathbf{n_3})/3, 
      \Pi = \sum_{i=1}^3 |\mathbf{e_i}|$ (sum of edge lengths).

Analytic integrals (I1,I2s,I2d) follow the project\textquotesingle{}s closed forms. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{Triangle_8cpp_source_l00062}{62}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{Triangle_8cpp_source}{Triangle.\+cpp}}.

\Hypertarget{classTriangle_aad716a04591b5c9af864d6c2a89d3a65}\index{Triangle@{Triangle}!containsPoint@{containsPoint}}
\index{containsPoint@{containsPoint}!Triangle@{Triangle}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{containsPoint()}{containsPoint()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classTriangle_aad716a04591b5c9af864d6c2a89d3a65} 
bool Triangle\+::contains\+Point (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const \mbox{\hyperlink{globals_8h_ae4271193b04e5d22237184d4fad54fb0}{rvec}} \&}]{p}{, }\item[{double}]{tol}{ = {\ttfamily 1e-\/6}}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Test whether a point lies on (or very near) the triangle. 

The point is projected to the triangle plane and tested using barycentric/simplex coordinates with tolerance {\ttfamily tol}.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em p} & Observation point. \\
\hline
{\em tol} & Tolerance for coplanarity and barycentric tests. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
true if p is on/near the facet; false otherwise.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Check if a point lies on/near the triangle (plane-\/project + barycentric).

Steps\+: 1) Reject if $ |(\mathbf{p} − \mathbf{n_0}) \cdot \mathbf{n}| > tol $ (off the plane). 2) Build an orthonormal basis \{unitX, unitY\} in the triangle plane. 3) Compute simplex (barycentric) coordinates (zeta) in 2D. 4) Accept if zeta\+\_\+i \texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}= −tol and sum(zeta) \texorpdfstring{$<$}{<}= 1+tol.
